name: Algebra I
description: Foundational algebra covering variables, equations, functions, graphing, systems, polynomials, and quadratics — the gateway to all higher mathematics.

modules.title: Variables & Algebraic Expressions
modules.objectives: Define variables; write and evaluate algebraic expressions; apply the vocabulary of algebra.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 1.1: Use the Language of Algebra
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Intro to Algebra: Variables & Expressions
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Writing Algebraic Expressions (20 problems)
modules.assignment: Write 10 real-life situations as algebraic expressions. Evaluate each expression for at least two different values of the variable.

modules.title: Order of Operations
modules.objectives: Apply PEMDAS/BODMAS to simplify numerical and algebraic expressions; distinguish evaluation order.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 1.1 (Order of Operations section)
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Introduction to Order of Operations
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Order of Operations Practice
modules.assignment: Create 5 multi-step expressions (with at least 4 operations each), solve them step by step showing every PEMDAS step.

modules.title: Properties of Real Numbers
modules.objectives: Identify and apply commutative, associative, distributive, identity, and inverse properties; simplify using properties.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 1.3–1.5: Real Number Properties
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Properties of Real Numbers
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Properties of Real Numbers
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Properties of Numbers Practice
modules.assignment: Identify which property justifies each of 10 algebraic steps in a provided simplification chain. Then simplify 5 expressions showing properties used.

modules.title: Solving One-Step & Two-Step Equations
modules.objectives: Use inverse operations to solve one-step and two-step linear equations; verify solutions.
modules.assignment: Solve 15 equations (mix of one-step and two-step). Write a word problem whose solution requires a two-step equation.

modules.title: Solving Multi-Step Equations & Literal Equations
modules.objectives: Solve equations with variables on both sides, parentheses, and fractions; rearrange literal equations for a specified variable.
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Variables on Both Sides
modules.assignment: Solve 10 multi-step equations. Rearrange 5 literal equations (e.g. solve A = lw for w; solve PV = nRT for T).

modules.title: Inequalities & Interval Notation
modules.objectives: Solve linear inequalities; graph solutions on a number line; write solutions in interval notation.
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Linear Inequalities & Interval Notation
modules.assignment: Solve and graph 10 inequalities. Convert 5 graph solutions into interval notation and set-builder notation.

modules.title: Introduction to Functions
modules.objectives: Define a function; identify domain and range; evaluate functions; use function notation; distinguish functions from non-functions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 3.5: Relations & Functions
modules.assignment: Given 5 relations, determine if each is a function. Evaluate f(x) for 10 different inputs across 3 functions. State the domain and range of each.

modules.title: Graphing Linear Functions
modules.objectives: Graph linear equations using a table of values, x/y-intercepts, and slope-intercept form; interpret graphs.
modules.practiceNote: Desmos Graphing Calculator — graph y = mx + b for various m and b values
modules.assignment: Graph 8 linear equations (using Desmos or pencil/graph paper). For each, identify the x-intercept, y-intercept, and state whether the slope is positive, negative, zero, or undefined.

modules.title: Slope & Rate of Change
modules.objectives: Calculate slope from two points and from a graph; interpret slope as rate of change in real-world contexts.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 3.4: Slope of a Line
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Slope of a Line
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Slope and Rate of Change
modules.assignment: Find the slope for 10 pairs of points. Find a real-world data set (e.g. population, temperature over time), plot 4 points, and calculate the average rate of change between consecutive points.

modules.title: Writing Linear Equations
modules.objectives: Write equations in slope-intercept form (y = mx + b), point-slope form, and standard form; convert between forms.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 4.1–4.2: Writing Equations of Lines
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Writing Equations of Lines
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Equation of a Line Practice
modules.assignment: Write the equation for 10 lines given various information (slope + point, two points, parallel/perpendicular conditions). Convert each to all three forms.

modules.title: Systems of Linear Equations
modules.objectives: Solve systems of equations by graphing, substitution, and elimination; interpret solutions (one, none, infinite).
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Solving Systems of Equations
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Systems of Equations Practice
modules.assignment: Solve 9 systems (3 by graphing, 3 by substitution, 3 by elimination). Write a word problem modeled by a system and solve it.

modules.title: Exponents & Polynomials
modules.objectives: Apply laws of exponents; classify polynomials; add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.
modules.assignment: Classify 5 polynomials by degree and number of terms. Perform 5 additions, 5 subtractions, and 5 multiplications of polynomials.

modules.title: Factoring Polynomials
modules.objectives: Factor out GCF; factor trinomials (leading coefficient = 1 and ≠ 1); use difference of squares and perfect square trinomials.
modules.assignment: Factor 15 polynomials using all learned methods. Check each answer by re-expanding.

modules.title: Quadratic Equations
modules.objectives: Solve quadratic equations by factoring, completing the square, and the quadratic formula; interpret discriminant.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e — Ch 10.1–10.3: Quadratic Equations
modules.assignment: Solve 12 quadratic equations (4 by factoring, 4 by completing the square, 4 by quadratic formula). For each, verify using the alternative method. Identify which equations have 0, 1, or 2 real solutions using the discriminant.

questions.question: Which property is illustrated by the statement: 3(x + 4) = 3x + 12?
questions.explanation: The Distributive Property states a(b + c) = ab + ac. Here 3 is distributed over (x + 4).

questions.question: Evaluate the expression 5x² − 3x + 2 when x = −2.
Simplify: 3 + 4² ÷ 2 − 1.
Solve for x: 3x − 7 = 14.
Solve for x: 2(x − 3) + 4 = 3x − 1.
The formula for the area of a trapezoid is A = ½h(b₁ + b₂). Solve for h.
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 2: 2A = h(b₁ + b₂). Divide by (b₁ + b₂): h = 2A/(b₁ + b₂).

questions.question: Solve and graph: −3x + 6 ≥ −9. Which interval represents the solution?
Which of the following relations is a function?
questions.explanation: A function requires each x-value to map to exactly one y-value. Only {(2,3),(3,3),(4,3)} has unique x-values.

questions.question: Given f(x) = 2x² − x + 3, what is f(−1)?
What is the slope of the line passing through (−3, 5) and (1, −3)?
Which equation represents a line with slope 3 passing through the point (2, −1)?
What is the solution to the system: y = 2x + 1 and y = −x + 7?
Simplify: (3x²y)³.
Multiply: (x + 3)(x − 5).
Factor completely: x² − 9.
questions.explanation: Difference of squares: a² − b² = (a + b)(a − b). So x² − 9 = (x + 3)(x − 3).

questions.question: Factor: x² + 7x + 12.
questions.explanation: Find two numbers that multiply to 12 and add to 7: 3 and 4. So (x + 3)(x + 4).

questions.question: Solve: x² − 5x + 6 = 0.
Use the quadratic formula to solve: 2x² + 3x − 2 = 0.
The discriminant of ax² + bx + c = 0 is b² − 4ac = −7. What does this tell you?
questions.explanation: When the discriminant is negative, there are no real solutions (the solutions are complex/imaginary).

questions.question: A ball is thrown upward. Its height in feet is h(t) = −16t² + 48t + 4, where t is in seconds. What is the initial height of the ball?
questions.explanation: The initial height is h(0) = −16(0)² + 48(0) + 4 = 4 feet.

questions.question: Evaluate 4x − 3 when x = 5.
Which expression represents "7 less than twice a number n"?
questions.explanation: "Twice a number" is 2n; "7 less than" means subtract 7 after.

questions.question: Simplify using order of operations: 5 + 3² × 2 − 4.
Which property justifies: 5(x + 2) = 5x + 10?
questions.explanation: The distributive property: a(b + c) = ab + ac.

questions.question: Solve: 4x + 5 = 21.
The formula P = 2l + 2w. Solve for w.
Solve and express in interval notation: 2x − 3 > 7.
Which set of ordered pairs represents a function?
questions.explanation: Each x-value maps to exactly one y-value. Only the first option has unique x-values.

questions.question: Given f(x) = x² − 2x + 1, find f(3).
Simplify: −3(2x − 4) + 5x.
A store sells pens for $2 each and notebooks for $5 each. Maria spends $31. She buys 3 notebooks. How many pens did she buy?
Solve: |x| = 7.
questions.explanation: Absolute value equals 7 when x = 7 or x = −7.

questions.question: What is the domain of f(x) = 1/(x − 3)?
questions.explanation: The denominator cannot be zero: x − 3 ≠ 0, so x ≠ 3.

questions.question: A taxi charges $3 flat fee plus $1.50 per mile. Write an equation for the total cost C in terms of miles m, then find the cost for 8 miles.

quizQuestions.question: What is the value of 3x + 5 when x = 2?
Which of the following is an algebraic expression?
quizQuestions.explanation: An algebraic expression contains variables and operations but no equals or inequality sign.

quizQuestions.question: "8 more than twice a number n" written as an expression is:
quizQuestions.explanation: "Twice a number" = 2n. "8 more than" = add 8. Result: 2n + 8.

quizQuestions.question: Evaluate x² − x when x = 4.
In the expression 5y + 3, the coefficient of y is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The coefficient is the number multiplying the variable. Here it is 5.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: 2 + 3 × 4.
quizQuestions.explanation: Multiply first: 3 × 4 = 12. Then add: 2 + 12 = 14.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: (2 + 3)².
quizQuestions.explanation: Parentheses first: 2 + 3 = 5. Then exponent: 5² = 25.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: 8 ÷ 2 + 3 × 2.
quizQuestions.explanation: Left to right for × and ÷: 8 ÷ 2 = 4; 3 × 2 = 6; 4 + 6 = 10.

quizQuestions.question: What does the "E" in PEMDAS stand for?
quizQuestions.explanation: PEMDAS: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication, Division, Addition, Subtraction.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: 3 + 4² ÷ 2 − 1.
Which property states a + b = b + a?
quizQuestions.explanation: The commutative property: order of addition (or multiplication) does not matter.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify using the distributive property: 4(x + 3).
Which is an example of the associative property?
quizQuestions.explanation: Associative: grouping of numbers can change without affecting the result.

quizQuestions.question: What is the additive inverse of −8?
quizQuestions.explanation: The additive inverse of a number is its opposite. −8 + 8 = 0.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: 3(2x + 1) − 2(x − 4).
Solve: x + 9 = 14.
quizQuestions.explanation: Subtract 9 from both sides: x = 14 − 9 = 5.

quizQuestions.question: Solve: 5x = 35.
quizQuestions.explanation: Divide both sides by 5: x = 35 ÷ 5 = 7.

quizQuestions.question: Solve: 2x − 3 = 7.
Solve: x/3 + 1 = 4.
Which equation has the solution x = −4?
quizQuestions.explanation: x + 4 = 0 → x = −4. The others give positive solutions.

quizQuestions.question: Solve: 4x − 1 = 2x + 7.
Solve A = ½bh for b.
quizQuestions.explanation: 2A = bh; b = 2A/h.

quizQuestions.question: Solve: 5 − 3x = 2x − 10.
Solve: x + 4 > 9.
quizQuestions.explanation: Subtract 4: x > 5.

quizQuestions.question: Solve: −2x ≤ 8. (Remember: dividing by a negative flips the inequality)
quizQuestions.explanation: Divide by −2 and flip: x ≥ −4.

quizQuestions.question: What is the interval notation for x < 3?
quizQuestions.explanation: x < 3 means all values less than 3 (not including 3), so open parenthesis: (−∞, 3).

quizQuestions.question: Solve: 3x − 2 ≥ 7.
Which graph represents x > −2?
options: Open circle at −2, arrow pointing right
Closed circle at −2, arrow pointing right
Open circle at −2, arrow pointing left
Closed circle at 2, arrow pointing left
quizQuestions.answer: Open circle at −2, arrow pointing right
quizQuestions.explanation: Strict inequality (>) uses an open circle; "greater than" means arrow points right.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function?
quizQuestions.explanation: x = 1 maps to both 2 and 3, violating the function rule.

quizQuestions.question: Given f(x) = 3x − 2, find f(4).
The set of all valid input values for a function is called the:
quizQuestions.explanation: Domain is the set of all possible x-values (inputs) for a function.

quizQuestions.question: The vertical line test determines whether a graph is a:
quizQuestions.explanation: If any vertical line crosses the graph more than once, it is not a function.

quizQuestions.question: If f(x) = x² + 1, what is the range for x ∈ {−2, 0, 2}?
quizQuestions.explanation: f(−2)=5, f(0)=1, f(2)=5. Range (unique values): {1, 5}.

quizQuestions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 4x − 6?
quizQuestions.explanation: The y-intercept is the point where x = 0: y = −6, so (0, −6).

quizQuestions.question: What is the x-intercept of y = 2x − 8?
quizQuestions.explanation: Set y = 0: 0 = 2x − 8; x = 4. x-intercept: (4, 0).

quizQuestions.question: What does the graph of y = 5 look like?
quizQuestions.explanation: y = constant is always a horizontal line.

quizQuestions.question: Which equation has a steeper slope: y = 3x + 1 or y = ½x − 2?
quizQuestions.explanation: Steepness is determined by |slope|. |3| > |½|, so y = 3x + 1 is steeper.

quizQuestions.question: A line passes through (0, 3) and (2, 7). What is its equation?
quizQuestions.explanation: Slope = (7−3)/(2−0) = 2. y-intercept = 3. Equation: y = 2x + 3.

quizQuestions.question: Find the slope between (1, 3) and (4, 9).
What is the slope of a horizontal line?
quizQuestions.explanation: A horizontal line has no vertical change, so slope = 0/run = 0.

quizQuestions.question: What is the slope of a vertical line?
quizQuestions.explanation: A vertical line has no horizontal change, so slope = rise/0 = undefined.

quizQuestions.question: A car travels 150 miles in 3 hours. What is its rate of change (slope)?
quizQuestions.explanation: Rate of change = 150 ÷ 3 = 50 miles per hour.

quizQuestions.question: What type of slope does a line going from lower-left to upper-right have?
quizQuestions.explanation: A line rising from left to right has a positive slope.

quizQuestions.question: Write the equation of a line with slope 3 and y-intercept −2.
quizQuestions.explanation: Slope-intercept form: y = mx + b. m = 3, b = −2.

quizQuestions.question: Which form is y − 4 = 2(x − 1)?
quizQuestions.explanation: Point-slope: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁). Here m = 2, point (1, 4).

quizQuestions.question: Two parallel lines have:
quizQuestions.explanation: Parallel lines never intersect and always have equal slopes.

quizQuestions.question: Write the equation through (0, −3) with slope 5.
quizQuestions.explanation: The point is the y-intercept (0, −3) and slope is 5: y = 5x − 3.

quizQuestions.question: A line perpendicular to y = 2x + 1 has slope:
quizQuestions.explanation: Perpendicular slopes are negative reciprocals. Reciprocal of 2 is ½; negate it: −½.

quizQuestions.question: What is the solution to a system of equations?
options: The point where two lines intersect
quizQuestions.answer: The point where two lines intersect
quizQuestions.explanation: The solution is the ordered pair (x, y) that satisfies both equations simultaneously.

quizQuestions.question: How many solutions does a system of two parallel lines have?
quizQuestions.explanation: Parallel lines never intersect, so there is no solution.

quizQuestions.question: Solve by substitution: y = x + 2 and x + y = 8.
quizQuestions.explanation: Substitute: x + (x+2) = 8; 2x = 6; x = 3. y = 5.

quizQuestions.question: Solve by elimination: 2x + y = 7 and x − y = 2.
quizQuestions.explanation: Add equations: 3x = 9; x = 3. Then y = 7 − 6 = 1.

quizQuestions.question: A system where both equations are the same line has:
quizQuestions.explanation: Identical (coincident) lines overlap completely — every point is a solution.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: x³ × x⁴.
quizQuestions.explanation: When multiplying same base: add exponents. x³⁺⁴ = x⁷.

quizQuestions.question: Simplify: (x²)⁵.
quizQuestions.explanation: Power rule: (xᵃ)ᵇ = xᵃᵇ. (x²)⁵ = x¹⁰.

quizQuestions.question: What is the degree of the polynomial 3x⁴ − 2x² + x − 5?
quizQuestions.explanation: The degree is the highest exponent. The leading term is 3x⁴, so degree = 4.

quizQuestions.question: Multiply: (x + 2)(x + 5).
quizQuestions.explanation: FOIL: x² + 5x + 2x + 10 = x² + 7x + 10.

quizQuestions.question: What is the GCF of 12x³ + 8x²?
quizQuestions.explanation: GCF of 12 and 8 is 4; GCF of x³ and x² is x². So GCF = 4x².

quizQuestions.question: Factor: x² + 8x + 15.
quizQuestions.explanation: Find two numbers that multiply to 15 and add to 8: 3 and 5.

quizQuestions.question: Factor: x² − 25.
quizQuestions.explanation: Difference of squares: x² − 25 = (x + 5)(x − 5).

quizQuestions.question: Factor: x² − 6x + 9.
quizQuestions.explanation: Perfect square trinomial: (x − 3)² = x² − 6x + 9.

quizQuestions.question: Factor completely: 3x² + 6x.
quizQuestions.explanation: Factor out GCF = 3x: 3x(x + 2).

quizQuestions.question: What is the standard form of a quadratic equation?
quizQuestions.explanation: Standard form: ax² + bx + c = 0, where a ≠ 0.

quizQuestions.question: Solve by factoring: x² − 7x + 12 = 0.
quizQuestions.explanation: Factor: (x − 3)(x − 4) = 0. Solutions: x = 3 or x = 4.

quizQuestions.question: Use the quadratic formula to solve: x² + 4x − 5 = 0.
quizQuestions.explanation: a=1, b=4, c=−5. Discriminant = 36. x = (−4 ± 6)/2 → x = 1 or x = −5.

quizQuestions.question: The discriminant b² − 4ac = 0. This means the quadratic has:
options: Exactly one real root (a repeated root)
quizQuestions.answer: Exactly one real root (a repeated root)
quizQuestions.explanation: When discriminant = 0, the quadratic touches the x-axis at exactly one point.

quizQuestions.question: Which quadratic has solutions x = 2 and x = −3?
